input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:

and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings

data.reasoningText:
The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate it to the modern `.runsettings` format after upgrading MSTest packages. Let me look at their project files to understand their current setup, then provide migration guidance.

Let me first look at what they have in their project.

data.content: `.testsettings` files are **not supported** in modern MSTest (v2+). You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's the equivalent for your configuration:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- Test timeout: 30 seconds per test -->
  <MSTest>
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <!-- Code coverage data collector -->
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.Coverage.DynamicCoverageDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content: Now wire it up in your project file so it's used automatically:

Here's a summary of the migration:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout></MSTest>` |
| Deployment/AssemblyResolution | `<DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>` (not needed for SDK-style projects) |
| Code Coverage DataCollector | Same concept, updated URI (`/2.0`) under `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` |

**Key points:**
- The old `.testsettings` can be deleted — it's silently ignored in MSTest v3.
- `RunSettingsFilePath` in the `.csproj` auto-applies the settings (or pass `--settings local.runsettings` to `dotnet test`).
- For per-test timeouts you can also use `[Timeout(30000)]` on individual methods.
